summary: Set 072 Session 3 of 4 -- the cross-run remediation aggregator. Ran on claude-opus-4-8/high. DELIVERED (all additive over the S2 module; REUSES merge_findings per stable key -- no re-implemented merge; S1 matrix seam + S2 per-run report/validator paths byte-for-byte untouched): added to ai_router/verification_only_app.py -- (1) aggregate_remediation_reports(reports, *, generated_at): rolls up N per-run remediation-report dicts over ONE target into a single remediation backlog. Groups findings across runs by stable defectKey (keyed -> one entry; unkeyed -> its own single-run group, never corroborates = the same safe over-split as within a run); for each group it RE-RUNS the Set-070 merge_findings (push/pull contributor lists reconstructed from each run's contributors) so provenance (push-only/pull-only/both = UNION of surfaces across runs), MAX severity, surface-union, and verbatim contributors are identical semantics to the per-run report. Each backlog finding is annotated with corroboration (= count of DISTINCT runs that surfaced it = the cross-config confidence/priority signal) + the run refs ({index, committedRef, generatedAt}) that surfaced it. Ordered severity-desc (primary) then corroboration-desc (secondary priority), stable within a tie. GUARD: refuses an empty set (VerificationOnlyError), a report with a missing/empty target, and a MIXED-TARGET set (MixedTargetError) -- a backlog spans exactly one target by construction. provenanceComplete + pushUnkeyed/pullUnkeyed summed across runs. (2) validate_remediation_backlog: pure-Python, NEVER raises, L-066-1 produce<->validate parity -- closed envelope, int-not-bool schemaVersion, runCount == len(runs), provenanceComplete<->unkeyed consistency, and each finding validated by the SHARED dual_surface_verify._validate_merged_finding on a stripped core copy (so every provenance invariant -- a 'both' finding must be keyed + cover both surfaces -- holds) PLUS the backlog-only corroboration/runs annotation. (3) render_remediation_backlog_markdown (ASCII-only, cp1252-safe) + write_backlog (json + md utf-8). (4) CLI 'aggregate' subcommand (--report repeatable + --out; validates each input via validate_remediation_report, returns int, ASCII status). Plus docs/remediation-backlog-schema.md (orchestrator-facing reference; validator-wins parity; one-target-by-construction + cross-config-corroboration framing) + __all__ exports. TESTS (+23 over S2's 20 = 43 in the file, all hermetic fixtures, NO metered calls): corroboration across runs (D1 push-only in run0 + pull-only in run1 -> ONE 'both', max-severity Critical, corroboration 2, both run refs) + single-run corroboration 1 + severity-then-corroboration ranking + unkeyed-never-corroborates (2 split entries, provenanceComplete False, pushUnkeyed 2) + mixed-target/empty/missing-target rejection + helper-validity sanity; validator negatives: non-object, bad-schema (bool-not-int), bad-kind, runCount mismatch, corroboration mismatch, duplicate run refs, stray run index, bad run-ref (bool index), unexpected-top-key, provenance inconsistency, and reuse-of-_validate_merged_finding-invariants (a 'both' stripped of its pull contributor is rejected); CLI: writes backlog json+md under real files + ASCII md + mixed-target nonzero + no-reports nonzero + invalid-report nonzero. SESSION VERIFICATION: cross-provider, routed_gate REQUIRED (blast-radius cross-artifact/shared-schema + multi-module {ai_router, docs} + breadth 5 files >=4); routed session-verification to gpt-5-4 (cross-provider from the anthropic orchestrator). R1 ISSUES_FOUND, 2 Major validator-parity findings (~$0.149): (F2) corroboration only checked == len(runs), not DISTINCT runs -- a duplicated/stray run ref could inflate the confidence signal; (F1) validate_remediation_backlog enforced only the one-way provenanceComplete check (pc=True => no unkeyed), not the converse (pc=False with zero unkeyed). ADOPTED F2 IN FULL: validator now requires each finding's run indices DISTINCT, each a member of the top-level runs roll-up, and corroboration == the DISTINCT count (the Set-070 cross-field-invariant precedent the S2 surfaces-distinctness fix already set); +2 negative tests (duplicate run refs, stray index); schema doc corroboration/runs note updated to state the distinct+membership invariant. DECLINED F1 with cross-provider-confirmed reasoning: it is the SAME over-reach gpt-5-4 raised in S2 R1 against validate_remediation_report/_validate_cell, adjudicated and DECLINED there with the S2 R2 verifier judging the decline SOUND; all three canonical sibling validators (validate_comparison_artifact, validate_remediation_report, _validate_cell) deliberately enforce only the one-way check, so adding the converse to the backlog validator alone would make it stricter than + inconsistent with its siblings; the rejected shape (pc=False on a fully-keyed backlog) is CONSERVATIVE not corrupting (downstream reads the unique view as an upper bound, the safe over-split direction -- only a false pc=True corrupts, which stays rejected); and it is NEVER producer-emitted (the producer derives pc + both counts from one merge result). R2 VERIFIED (~$0.022) -- F2 fix confirmed + the F1 decline judged sound. s3-verification.md (R1) + s3-verification-r2.md (R2) saved raw, never edited. SUITE: full ai_router GREEN 2232 passed / 5 skipped (-m 'not e2e'; the 5 are the Set 069 S4 real-Podman-on-Windows by-design skips; +2 from this session's R2 fix over the 2230 mid-session baseline). NEXT S4 routed rec (route analysis -> s3-next-session.md, per L-064-6): claude-opus-4-8/high (primary) -- the final, release-shipping session (synthesis docs + 0.25.0->0.26.0 PyPI release + dogfood + first external harvester run + close) prioritizes capability + release-judgment over cost; openai/gpt-5-4 the non-anthropic tier-3 alternative. The operator chooses.

reason.specifics: Routed analysis (route task_type=analysis -> s3-next-session.md, per L-064-6) recommends claude-opus-4-8/high for Session 4 of 4 -- the FINAL, release-shipping session (synthesis updates to verification-surface-strategy.md + pull-verifier.md + a candidate lesson; ai_router minor bump 0.25.0 -> 0.26.0 and the PyPI release per the publish runbook; change-log.md + next-SET recommendation; the dogfood of the new matrix mode over this set's own diff; and the FIRST real external run on ../dabbler-access-harvester confirming the Gemini-pull-under-strong-framing cell returns a verdict not silence). This session ships a production release and runs metered cross-provider verification + a metered external matrix run, so capability and release-judgment outweigh cost; the highest-tier model is the appropriate choice. openai/gpt-5-4 is the non-anthropic tier-3 alternative the routed analysis offered. The operator chooses.
